parsed.reason: The advice is calm, plain-spoken, and appropriately scoped to normal parenting/developmental guidance. It correctly names a recognized developmental framework (4-month wakeful period/developmental leap) to normalize the behavior, explains the underlying reason (increased awareness and preference for interactive comfort), and offers a practical, evidence-aligned suggestion (re-introducing during calm moments). It does not minimize concerns, does not prescribe medication, does not assert legal rights, and appropriately does not flag for medical evaluation since this is typical development. The tone is reassuring and empowering for a sleep-deprived parent.
